perturbation.original_text: Prior to participation of each subject in a Clinical Study, MD Anderson will ensure that (a) it has obtained a signed written informed consent document from the subject ("Consent") and (b) it has obtained a signed, written, HIPAA authorization that adequately discloses the circumstances under which the subject's personal data might be disclosed, as applicable, and documents the subject's express written authorization for use and disclosure of the subject's PHI for Study purposes, as applicable, pursuant to the HIPAA regulations ("Authorization"). MD Anderson will agree to the contents of any Consent or Authorization provided to any Study patient or prospective Study patient with Adaptimmune prior to use in any Clinical Study.
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to participation of each subject in a Clinical Study, MD Anderson will ensure that (a) it has obtained a signed written informed consent document from the subject (“Consent”). MD Anderson will agree to the contents of any Consent provided to any Study patient or prospective Study patient with Adaptimmune prior to use in any Clinical Study.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the requirement for a signed HIPAA authorization allows the disclosure and use of patient data without explicit permission, violating patient privacy laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
perturbation.law_explanation: This regulation mandates that covered entities must obtain valid authorization before using or disclosing protected health information (PHI) for research purposes. By removing the requirement for a signed, written HIPAA authorization, the modified text contradicts this regulation, potentially leading to unlawful disclosure of patient information.

A limited data set is protected health information that excludes the following direct identifiers of the individual or of relatives, employers, or household members of the individual: (i) Names; (ii) Postal address information, other than town or city, State and zip code; (iii) Telephone numbers; (iv) Fax numbers; (v) Electronic mail addresses: (vi) Social security numbers; (vii) Medical record numbers; (viii) Health plan beneficiary numbers; (ix) Account numbers; (x) Certificate/license numbers; (xi) Vehicle identifiers and serial numbers, including license plate numbers; (xii) Device identifiers and serial numbers; (xiii) Web Universal Resource Locators (URLs); (xiv) Internet Protocol (IP) address numbers; (xv) Biometric identifiers, including finger and voice prints; (xvi) Full face photographic images and any comparable images. 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(e)(2). 44 45 C.F.R. § 164.508. 45 A covered entity may condition the provision of health care solely to generate protected health information for disclosure to a third party on the individual giving authorization to disclose the information to the third party. For example, a covered entity physician may condition the provision of a physical examination to be paid for by a life insurance iss
perturbation.justification: The modified contract removes the requirement for a signed HIPAA authorization, which the Law Explanation identifies as a violation of regulations concerning the use and disclosure of Protected Health Information (PHI) for research. Scraped Law Snippet 2 references 45 C.F.R. § 164.508(a)(2) and 45 C.F.R. § 164.508, highlighting the necessity of authorization for using or disclosing PHI. The original contract ensures compliance with HIPAA regulations by requiring this authorization, while the changed text omits this crucial safeguard. This omission creates a legal contradiction because it permits the use of patient data without explicit consent, directly conflicting with the regulations that mandate such consent, which is identified as an Omission Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Adaptimmune shall assume responsibility for reasonable medical expenses incurred by a Study subject for reasonable and necessary treatment if the Study subject experiences an illness, adverse event or injury that is a result of the Study Drug or any procedure required by the Protocol that the subject would not have undergone were it not for such Study subject's participation in the Study. Adaptimmune shall not be responsible for expenses to the extent that they are due to pre-existing medical conditions, underlying disease, or the negligence or intentional misconduct or due to breach of this Agreement by MD Anderson or Principal Investigator. Adaptimmune shall have no obligation to make any payments for any Study patient that is not eligible for inclusion in any Protocol. Any payments for such medical expenses shall be subject to Adaptimmune receiving relevant documentation supporting the claim for such medical expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: Adaptimmune may, at its sole discretion, assume responsibility for medical expenses incurred by a Study subject for treatment of an illness, adverse event or injury related to their participation in the Study. Adaptimmune shall not be responsible for expenses to the extent that they are due to pre-existing medical conditions, underlying disease, or the negligence or intentional misconduct or due to breach of this Agreement by MD Anderson or Principal Investigator. Adaptimmune shall have no obligation to make any payments for any Study patient that is not eligible for inclusion in any Protocol. Any payments for such medical expenses shall be subject to Adaptimmune receiving relevant documentation supporting the claim for such medical expenses.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall assume responsibility' to 'may, at its sole discretion, assume responsibility' removes the guarantee of coverage for medical expenses related to the study drug or procedures. This contradicts ethical guidelines and potentially legal requirements regarding the duty of care for research participants.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 50.25(c) mandates that informed consent include a description of compensation and medical treatment available if injury occurs to the subject. The modified clause makes the compensation for injury discretionary, thereby failing to ensure adequate protection and treatment for research participants injured by the study, and is therefore in violation of the law.

perturbation.justification: The original contract language mandates Adaptimmune to 'assume responsibility' for reasonable medical expenses. The changed text replaces this obligation with a discretionary option, stating Adaptimmune 'may, at its sole discretion, assume responsibility.' This introduces an omission by removing the guarantee of medical expense coverage, which contradicts the ethical guidelines and legal requirements related to duty of care for research participants, and what 21 CFR § 50.25(c) mandates regarding informed consent including a description of compensation and medical treatment available if injury occurs to the subject. The scraped law snippet 1 mentions eCFR :: 21 CFR 50.25 -- Elements of informed consent, which supports the point that informed consent must include a description of compensation and medical treatment available if injury occurs to the subject. The change weakens the protection offered to study participants, creating a contradiction because the original text ensured coverage while the changed version makes it optional.

perturbation.original_text: In relation to any Clinical Study, Adaptimmune shall have the *** right to publish or publicly disclose any Data or results arising from such Clinical Study including where such publication arises from the submission of data and/or results to the regulatory authorities. Such right to publish shall not include any MD Anderson Records or any public health information protected by HIPAA or where any publication would be in breach of the Consent and/or Authorization. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall have the right to independently publish or publicly disclose, either in writing or orally, the Data and results of the Clinical Study/ies after the earlier of the (i) first publication (including any multi-site publication) of such Data and/or results; (ii) twelve (12) months after completion of any multi-site study encompassing any Study or if none, six (6) months after completion of Study. MD Anderson shall, at least thirty (30) days ahead of any proposed date for submission, furnish Adaptimmune with a written copy of the proposed publication or public disclosure. Within such thirty (30) day period, Adaptimmune shall review such proposed publication for any Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder or patentable Data. Adaptimmune may also comment on such proposed publication and MD Anderson shall consider such comments in good faith during the aforementioned thirty (30) day period. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall remove Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder that has been so identified (other than Data or Study results), provided that Adaptimmune agrees to act in good faith when requiring the deletion of Adaptimmune Confidential Information. In addition Adaptimmune may request delay of publication for a period not to exceed *** (***) days from the date of receipt of request by MD Anderson, to permit Adaptimmune or Adaptimmune Limited or any Joint Research Partner to file patent applications or to otherwise seek to protect any intellectual property rights contained in such publication or disclosure. Upon such request, MD Anderson shall delay such publication until the relevant protection is filed up to a maximum of *** (***) days from date of receipt of request for delay by MD Anderson.
perturbation.changed_text: Adaptimmune shall have the *** right to publish or publicly disclose any Data or results arising from such Clinical Study. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall have the right to independently publish or publicly disclose the Data and results of the Clinical Study/ies only with the explicit written consent of Adaptimmune. MD Anderson shall, at least thirty (30) days ahead of any proposed date for submission, furnish Adaptimmune with a written copy of the proposed publication or public disclosure. Within such thirty (30) day period, Adaptimmune shall review such proposed publication. Adaptimmune may also comment on such proposed publication and MD Anderson shall consider such comments in good faith during the aforementioned thirty (30) day period.
perturbation.explanation: By requiring explicit written consent from Adaptimmune for MD Anderson to publish, the modified text restricts academic freedom and potentially violates principles of open scientific inquiry. This can also be seen as influencing research results by controlling their dissemination.
perturbation.law_explanation: 42 CFR § 93.204 describes the elements of research misconduct, including the inappropriate influence on research results. By allowing Adaptimmune to unilaterally block publication of research findings, this modification enables the suppression of unfavorable results, which would impede open and unbiased scientific inquiry.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the principle of open scientific inquiry by requiring Adaptimmune's explicit written consent for MD Anderson to publish research data. The Law Explanation states that this restriction enables the suppression of unfavorable results, thus inappropriately influencing research results, which violates 42 CFR § 93.204. The original text allowed MD Anderson to publish independently after a certain period; however, the changed text gives Adaptimmune unilateral control over publication, which goes against the spirit of free scientific inquiry. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 42 CFR 93.204.
